title: 10 best herbal tinctures for shingles
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for shingles are liquid extracts made from plants and herbs that have anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and analgesic properties. They help alleviate the symptoms of shingles by reducing pain, itching, and inflammation. For example, St. John's Wort and Lemon Balm tinctures can soothe the skin and reduce discomfort. Additionally, Echinacea and Goldenseal tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and redness. By using these herbal tinctures, people with shingles can experience improved sleep quality, reduced pain levels, and a faster recovery time, ultimately improving their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with shingles because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has been shown to reduce pain and discomfort associated with shingles by suppressing inflammation and alleviating nerve damage. The bioavailability of curcumin in tincture form allows it to quickly enter the bloodstream and target affected areas, providing rapid relief from symptoms such as burning, itching, and numbness. Additionally, turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect against viral reactivation, promoting a faster recovery and reducing the risk of future outbreaks.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has potent anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce pain and swelling associated with shingles, making it a valuable natural remedy for managing symptoms.

Antiviral properties: Turmeric has been shown to have antiviral activity against the herpes simplex virus (HSV-1), which causes shingles, helping to slow down the replication of the virus and potentially reducing the duration and severity of the outbreak.

Wound-healing properties: Turmeric's antimicrobial and antioxidant effects can help promote wound healing by reducing bacterial growth and inflammation, promoting collagen synthesis, and improving skin elasticity, making it beneficial for treating the painful blisters associated with shingles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Turmeric roots have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues like shingles, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.

Rhizomes: The rhizome is the underground stem of the turmeric plant, which contains the most active compounds responsible for its medicinal properties, making it an ideal part for making tinctures to combat shingles.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried turmeric roots from a reputable source and measure out 2 ounces per quart of liquid.
Combine the turmeric roots with 80% ethanol or vodka in a glass jar, leaving one inch of headspace.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for two to three weeks, shaking daily for eight to ten minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, label and store in the refrigerator for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help to alleviate the painful symptoms associated with this condition. The tincture's active compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making it a natural remedy for soothing the discomfort caused by shingles. Additionally, ginger's antimicrobial properties may also help to combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing the duration and severity of the outbreak.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can help reduce inflammation and alleviate the painful symptoms of shingles, including itching, burning, and numbness.

Antiviral properties: Ginger has been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including herpes simplex virus (the virus that causes shingles), which can help combat the infection and shorten the duration of symptoms.
Pain-relieving properties: Ginger's ability to block pain receptors and reduce inflammation makes it a natural analgesic that can help alleviate the severe nerve pain associated with shingles.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that help alleviate shingles symptoms.
Roots: Contains a high concentration of gingerols and other active compounds that can be extracted to create a potent tincture for reducing pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger rhizomes for a minimum of two pounds.
Clean the harvested ginger thoroughly with distilled water and gently scrub off any dirt.
Chop the cleaned ginger into small pieces and combine them with 80% proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for six weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily allowing the tincture to mature.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with shingles because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with shingles. Additionally, St John's Wort has antimicrobial properties that may help combat the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles, reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks. By using St John's Wort tincture as a natural remedy, individuals can find relief from the discomfort and symptoms of shingles without relying on pharmaceutical medications.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: St. John's Wort has been shown to inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles, reducing its ability to cause further inflammation and pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort tinctures have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce swelling, itching, and discomfort associated with shingles rashes.

Pain-relieving properties: The alkaloids found in St. John's Wort tinctures have analgesic and anesthetic properties, which can help alleviate the severe pain and numbness often experienced during shingles outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing bioactive compounds, roots are used to create a concentrated extract that targets the underlying causes of shingles.
Flowers: Rich in hyperforin and hypericin, flowers are used to create a tincture that specifically addresses the painful and inflammatory symptoms of shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum flowers at peak blooming time for optimal potency.
Cleanly chop the harvested flowers and combine them in a clean glass jar with vodka or everclear.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place allowing it to steep for 6-8 weeks to extract active compounds.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass dropper bottles and label with date prepared dosage instructions and storage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with the condition. The tannins present in sage have been shown to inhibit the replication of herpes viruses, including varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles. Additionally, sage's antioxidant properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting a faster recovery from shingles outbreaks. As a natural remedy, sage tinctures can provide relief from shingles symptoms without the use of pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Sage tincture's antiviral compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing its replication and spread.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, like camphor and borneol, can help alleviate pain, itching, and swelling associated with shingles rash and blisters.

Antioxidant properties: Sage tincture's antioxidant capacity can help neutralize oxidative stress and free radicals generated by the viral infection, promoting tissue repair and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions and inflammation, making them a popular choice for shingles.
Buds: The buds of the sage plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated skin.
Seeds: Sage seeds contain saponins, which have been shown to have antiviral properties that may help combat the herpes virus that causes shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried salvia officinalis leaves and flowers from a trusted source weighing approximately 30g per batch.
Chop the plant material into smaller pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped plant material with 200ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar ensuring the mixture is submerged under the liquid.
Seal the jar tightly and allow it to sit for 14 days at room temperature allowing for extraction to occur.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with shingles because of its natural antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including those that cause shingles. Additionally, lavender's calming effects can help alleviate the intense pain and discomfort associated with shingles, promoting relaxation and reducing stress. By addressing both the viral and inflammatory aspects of the condition, English lavender tinctures may provide relief from the symptoms of shingles and support the body's natural healing process.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: English lavender's antiviral properties can help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as linalool, can reduce inflammation and pain associated with shingles, providing relief from itching, burning, and discomfort.

Anxiolytic properties: English lavender's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress, which are common comorbidities with shingles, promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing overall symptom burden.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm nerve pain associated with shingles.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are rich in antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing of skin lesions caused by shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and stems.
Combine one part lavender material with three parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dry place indefinitely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with shingles because of its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The menthol in peppermint oil has been shown to reduce pain and itching associated with shingles, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a sense of calm. Additionally, the antioxidant and antimicrobial properties of peppermint help to combat viral infections, potentially shortening the duration and severity of the shingles outbreak. As a result, incorporating herbal peppermint tinctures into one's treatment plan can provide effective natural support for managing shingle symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Peppermint's antiviral compounds, such as menthol and menthone, can help inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus (VZV), which causes shingles.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes in peppermint tincture may help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with shingle lesions, alleviating discomfort and pain.
Pain-relieving properties: Peppermint's menthol content can also provide topical analgesic effects, helping to numb the area affected by shingles and reduce itching and burning sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain a high concentration of menthol and other volatile oils, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Bark: The bark of the peppermint plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antiviral properties, helping to combat the herpes virus that causes shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves and dry them immediately in a cool dark place for 24 hours.
Combine 100g of dried peppermint leaves with 500ml of 45% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and steep it for 4 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids after 30 minutes.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 months before use with dosages of 20-40 drops 3 times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with shingles because it has a natural ability to reduce inflammation, pain, and itching associated with the condition. The antispasmodic properties of valerian root can help soothe and calm the nerves, which may become inflamed during an outbreak. Additionally, valerian's sedative effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress, common triggers for shingles symptoms. By targeting these underlying factors, herbal valerian tinctures provide natural relief from the discomfort and distress caused by shingles, allowing individuals to better manage their condition and recover more comfortably.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and Sedative Properties: Valerian tincture helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can alleviate the mental and emotional discomfort associated with shingles.

Anti-Inflammatory and Antiviral Properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and pain in shingles lesions, while its antiviral properties may inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles.
Pain-Relieving Properties: Valerian has been shown to have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, which can help alleviate the intense burning pain and itching associated with shingles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which have been shown to possess antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in alleviating shingles symptoms.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, of the valerian plant contains sesquiterpenes, which have been found to exhibit antiviral and analgesic activities, helping to reduce shingles-related pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after dew has dried.
Chop 500g of harvested root into small pieces and transfer to a clean glass jar.
Add 1000ml of 40% vodka or grain alcohol to the jar covering the root material completely.
Steep the mixture for 14 days, shaking the jar daily and then filter out plant material.
Transfer filtered liquid to dark glass bottles and store in cool dry place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with shingles because they contain natural compounds that target the underlying viral infection causing the outbreak. The antiviral properties of maypop have been shown to inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, which is responsible for shingles. Additionally, maypop's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce pain and itching associated with shingles. By addressing both the root cause of the infection and alleviating symptoms, maypop tinctures offer a holistic approach to managing shingles and promoting faster healing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and anti-pruritic properties: Maypop's flavonoids and glycosides help reduce inflammation, itching, and discomfort associated with shingles.
Anxiolytic and sedative effects: The herb's active compounds can calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that often exacerbate shingles symptoms.

Antiviral properties: Maypop contains antiviral compounds like flavonoids and alkaloids that may help inhibit the replication of the herpes zoster virus, potentially reducing the severity and duration of shingles outbreaks.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Barks: The barks of the Maypop plant have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, including shingles, due to their antiseptic and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality passiflora incarnata flowers in the morning after the dew has dried, gathering at least one cupful for the batch.
Dry the collected flowers using a food dehydrator on low heat for 3-4 hours or air-drying them for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine the dried passiflora incarnata flowers with 70% vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the plant material is completely covered by the solvent.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and sedimentation of the herbal compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and labeling the product with date prepared, plant material used, and dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tinctures helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The herb has been used traditionally to treat skin conditions, including rashes and itches associated with shingles. Burdock root contains inulin, which stimulates the immune system to fight off viral infections, reducing the severity and duration of shingles outbreaks. Additionally, burdock's antioxidant compounds help soothe irritated skin, promoting healing and reducing scarring. By addressing both internal inflammation and external symptoms, burdock tinctures can provide relief from the discomfort and pain of shingles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Burdock's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce pain, swelling, and itching associated with shingles rash.
Antiviral: Burdock has antiviral compounds that may help suppress the varicella-zoster virus (VZV) that causes shingles, reducing the severity of symptoms.
Immune-modulating: Burdock's immunomodulatory effects can help regulate the immune response to VZV infection, potentially shortening the duration and severity of shingles episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that soothes inflammation and calms the nervous system, making them effective in treating shingles-related pain and discomfort.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds like polyacetylenes, which help reduce pain and swelling associated with shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried arctium lappa roots and cut them into small pieces for drying or grinding.
Dry the chopped arctium lappa root material in a warm, dark place for at least 24 hours or use a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours.
Combine 1 part dried arctium lappa root with 4 parts high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar and seal tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for at least 4 weeks to allow for maceration and extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids, and label as 1:5 arctium lappa tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with shingles because it has antiviral properties that target the herpes zoster virus, reducing its replication and spread. The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds also help soothe itching, burning, and pain associated with shingles. Additionally, elder tincture's immune-boosting abilities support the body's natural defenses against viral infections, promoting faster recovery and reduced risk of future outbreaks. By addressing the root causes of shingles, elder tinctures provide a holistic approach to managing this often debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Elderberry's antiviral compounds, such as anthocyanins and phenolic acids, help to combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing its replication and spread.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and polyphenols in elderberry tinctures have potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate pain, itching, and swelling associated with shingles lesions.

Immune system support: Elderberry's immunomodulatory activity helps to boost the body's natural defense against viral infections like shingles, enhancing the production of white blood cells and supporting the immune response.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to soothe and calm shingle-related pain and discomfort.
Barks: Containing saponins and phenolic acids that exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activity, supporting the body's natural defenses against shingles virus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Chop 250 grams of harvested plant material into small pieces using sharp scissors or pruning shears.
Combine chopped plant material with 625 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar covering completely.
Steep mixture for 14 days in a dark cool place away from direct sunlight and heat sources.
Strain tincture through cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding solids after pressing gently.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with shingles because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate the excruciating pain associated with shingles. The tincture's ability to reduce inflammation in the nerves can also help ease the discomfort caused by nerve damage. Additionally, maidenhair tree's antimicrobial properties may help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing its spread and severity. By addressing these symptoms, maidenhair tree tinctures may provide effective relief for individuals suffering from shingles.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Maidenhair Tree tincture contains compounds that have been shown to inhibit the replication of certain viruses, including herpes simplex virus (HSV), which is responsible for causing shingles.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The tannins and flavonoids present in Maidenhair Tree tincture may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with shingle outbreaks by inhibiting enzymes that promote inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content of Maidenhair Tree tincture can help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate symptoms of shingles such as burning, itching, and numbness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing pain associated with shingles.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginkgo biloba leaves and dry them thoroughly in a warm dark place for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Combine 20g of ground ginkgo biloba with 100ml of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all herb is fully submerged.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14-21 days, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction, and then strain through cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Transfer the resulting tincture into amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids, labeling and dating each bottle accurately, and store in a cool dark place for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and soothing properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and bisabolol, work to reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with shingles rash and blisters. Additionally, chamomile's natural calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress that often accompanies the condition, promoting a sense of relaxation and comfort during the healing process. By addressing both physical and emotional symptoms, herbal chamomile tinctures provide a holistic approach to managing shingles.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with shingles.

Antiviral properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in chamomile tincture have been shown to exhibit antiviral activity against certain viruses, including the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles.
Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Chamomile's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety, stress, and insomnia associated with shingles, promoting a restful sleep and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, chamomile roots are used to reduce pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Flowers: Chamomile flowers contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing itching and discomfort caused by shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for a total of 100 grams.
Chop the harvested flowers into small pieces and combine them with 40% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least 14 days and up to 30 days in a cool dark place.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid and use within 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the intense itching and burning associated with this condition. The marshmallow's mucilage also soothes and coats the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, marshmallow contains saponins, which have antiviral properties that may help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms. By taking an herbal marshmallow tincture regularly, individuals with shingles may experience faster healing and relief from painful symptoms.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow root contains a thick, gel-like substance called mucilage that helps to soothe and protect irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with shingles.

Antiviral activity: The tannins and flavonoids present in marshmallow tincture have been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which may help to combat the varicella-zoster virus responsible for shingles and reduce the severity of symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Marshmallow tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as beta-sitosterol and lupeol, can help to reduce swelling, redness, and pain associated with shingles, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilages, the roots provide soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate shingles symptoms.
Barks: Containing flavonoids and phenolic acids, the barks have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that can aid in reducing shingle-related pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis leaves and flowers in the early morning or evening for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to facilitate extraction of active compounds.
Combine one part chopped althaea officinalis with three parts 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate at room temperature for six weeks, shaking daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and discard solids, bottling the resulting liquid in dark glass containers.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. The glycyrrhizin component in licorice root has been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses, including the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles. Additionally, licorice's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce pain, itching, and burning associated with shingles. By reducing inflammation and inhibiting viral growth, herbal licorice tinctures may alleviate symptoms and promote faster healing of shingles lesions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with shingles rash and blisters.

Antiviral activity: The triterpenoids present in licorice root have been shown to exhibit antiviral properties, which may help combat the varicella-zoster virus that causes shingles and reduce the severity of symptoms.

Gastric protectant effects: Licorice root has been traditionally used to soothe stomach ulcers and inflammation, which is often a secondary complication of shingles due to medication side effects or nausea/vomiting associated with the condition; its gastric protectant properties can help alleviate these discomforts.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making it effective in soothing shingles-related pain and reducing inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain triterpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antiviral and antibacterial properties, helping to combat the herpes virus that causes shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried licorice root, glycyrrhiza glabra, in amounts of one pound for every 5 cups of vodka.
Chop the licorice root into small pieces and transfer them to a clean glass jar with an airtight lid.
Fill the jar with 5 cups of 80 proof vodka, leaving about 1 inch at the top, and seal the jar tightly.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking it daily, allowing the mixture to steep and extract its properties.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids, and store it in the refrigerator after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with shingles because of its potent antiviral properties, which have been shown to effectively combat the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles. The antimicrobial compounds present in thyme, such as carvacrol and thymol, help to reduce the severity and duration of shingles symptoms, including pain, itching, and rashes. Additionally, thyme tinctures may also help to boost the immune system, supporting the body's natural defense against infections and promoting faster recovery from shingles.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: Thyme's antiviral properties can help combat the varicella-zoster virus (VZV) that causes shingles, reducing the severity and duration of the outbreak.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce pain, swelling, and itching associated with shingles by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators.
Antioxidant activity: Thyme's antioxidants can help protect skin cells from oxidative stress caused by the viral infection, promoting wound healing and reducing the risk of scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with shingles.
Flowers: Containing volatile oils that have antiviral properties, which can help combat the viral nature of shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material using paper towels or clean cloth for 30 minutes.
Combine one part thymus vulgaris plant material with two parts vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a glass jar for 14 days.
Shake the mixture twice daily to ensure plant material is fully infused then strain through cheesecloth and discard solids after 2 hours.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature protected from light for 6 months or more.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with shingles because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tincture's flavonoids, such as luteolin and quercetin, have been shown to reduce pain and swelling associated with shingles, while also combating the underlying herpes zoster virus. Additionally, pot marigold's antiviral compounds may help shorten the duration of the outbreak and prevent future recurrences. By reducing inflammation and fighting off the virus, pot marigold tinctures provide natural relief for individuals suffering from this painful and debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Calendula's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce pain, swelling, and discomfort associated with shingles by decreasing inflammation in the affected area.
Antiviral: The antiviral properties of Calendula may help combat the Herpes Zoster virus that causes shingles, reducing its severity and duration.
Wound-healing: Calendula's wound-healing properties can promote faster recovery from the painful blisters and lesions characteristic of shingles, reducing the risk of infection and scarring.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making them effective in soothing and healing shingle lesions.
Leaves: Pot marigold leaves have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like eczema and psoriasis, and their inclusion in tinctures for shingles can help reduce inflammation and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers at peak bloom in full sun for optimal medicinal properties.
Cleanse and dry the harvested flowers by gently stirring them in a warm air current for several hours.
Combine 100 grams of dried calendula flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain and filter the liquid tincture through cheesecloth into dark glass bottles, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with shingles because its potent anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate the excruciating pain associated with this condition. The tincture's ability to reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected area can also speed up the healing process, allowing for a quicker recovery from the rash and blisters. Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant properties can help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by the virus that triggers shingles, further supporting the body's natural defenses against the condition.
remedy_properties: Diuretic Properties: Dandelion's diuretic effects can help reduce inflammation and relieve pressure on the nerve endings, which can reduce the severity of shingles pain.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: Dandelion contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol and triterpenoids, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with shingles, promoting faster healing.
Antiviral Properties: Dandelion's antiviral properties may help combat the herpes virus that causes shingles, reducing the severity of outbreaks and speeding up recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots help to soothe and calm the skin affected by shingles.
Fruits: Dandelion fruits contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antiviral properties that can aid in reducing the severity of shingles symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion flowers in early morning when they are fully open.
Weigh out 20 grams of dried taraxacum officinale roots and place them into clean glass jar.
Fill the jar completely with 95% ethanol vodka, ensuring all plant material is covered.
Store the mixture at room temperature for four weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng tinctures helps with shingles because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate the painful symptoms associated with this viral infection. The tincture's ability to boost the immune system can also help reduce the severity of the outbreak, while its adaptogenic properties can aid in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. By supporting the body's natural defense mechanisms, Siberian ginseng tinctures may help shorten the duration and intensity of shingles episodes, providing relief from discomfort and improving quality of life.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Siberian Ginseng helps the body adapt to stress, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with Shingles, such as pain, itching, and fatigue.
Immune system support: Siberian Ginseng has been shown to stimulate the immune system, which can help boost the body's natural defenses against the Varicella-Zoster virus that causes Shingles.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture contains compounds like polysaccharides and saponins that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and relieve discomfort associated with Shingles lesions.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, roots are extracted to create a concentrated solution that helps alleviate shingles symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stem of the plant is prized for its anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, making it effective in treating shingles-related pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried eleutherococcus senticosus roots in quantities of 250-500 grams and clean them thoroughly.
Chop the roots into small pieces and macerate them in high-proof vodka or everclear for 2 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for at least 6 months to allow sedimentation and maturation.
Shake the tincture well before use and administer 20-30 drops sublingually 3 times daily for relief from shingles symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tinctures helps with shingles because it contains eucalyptol, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that reduces pain and swelling associated with the condition. The menthol content in the tincture also helps to numb the skin, providing relief from itching and burning sensations common with shingles. Additionally, the antiviral properties of eucalyptus may help shorten the duration and severity of the outbreak by reducing the spread of the herpes zoster virus that causes shingles. By targeting multiple aspects of the condition, Tasmanian blue gum tinctures offer a natural and effective approach to managing shingles symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antiviral activity: The tincture's antiviral compounds can help inhibit the replication of the varicella-zoster virus, which causes shingles, thereby reducing its spread and severity.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum tincture has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling, pain, and itching associated with shingles, promoting a faster recovery.

Antioxidant activity: The tincture's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from oxidative stress caused by the virus, reducing the risk of postherpetic neuralgia (PHN), a common complication of shingles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of Tasmanian Blue Gum have been traditionally used to treat shingles due to their anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties.
Rhyzomes: The underground stems (rhyzomes) of the plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenoids, which have shown potent activity against herpes viruses, including those that cause shingles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves from a trusted source ensuring 50 grams of leaf material for each batch.
Chop the leaves into smaller pieces using scissors or a herb cutter to release essential oils and flavor compounds.
Combine chopped leaves with 1000 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar allowing 4 weeks for infusion time.
Shake the mixture daily during the infusion period ensuring uniform extraction of active constituents from plant material.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids and transferring to dark glass bottles for storage.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat shingles with herbal tinctures is by combining a few key botanicals. Valerian root tincture helps alleviate pain and itching, while St. John's Wort tincture soothes inflammation and promotes wound healing. Echinacea tincture enhances immune function to combat the virus that causes shingles. Take 20-30 drops of each tincture three times a day, diluted in water or mixed with honey for ease of consumption.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to shingles that are treated with herbal tinctures are postherpetic neuralgia, herpes simplex, and genital herpes. Herbal tinctures such as those made from St. John's Wort, lemon balm, and chamomile have been traditionally used to soothe and calm the pain and discomfort associated with these conditions. These herbs possess anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms.
